parts.answer: • calculation of mass of pentane • calculation of moles of pentane • calculation of moles of carbon dioxide • calculation of volume of carbon dioxide in cm (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation m = (15.0 × 0.626 =) 9.39 (g) n = (9.39 ÷ 72 =) 0.13042 (mol) n = (0.13042 × 5 =) 0.65208 (mol) V = (0.65208 × 24 × 10 = ) 15650 (cm ) Correct answer with no working scores (4) TE at each stage Ignore rounding throughout Ignore SF except 1SF Ignore intermediate units (4) (Total for Question 21 = 14 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • the energy required • to remove one electron from each atom in a mole of atoms or to produce one mole of singly charged positive ions from one mole of atoms • in the gaseous state (1) (1) (1) Allow enthalpy/energy change Do not award energy given out Ignore any equations (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• equation (1) Example of equation Li + → Li 2+ + e( − ) or Li + − e( − ) → Li 2+ Ignore state symbols (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • helium only has two electrons / helium does not have a third electron to lose (1) Allow It has a configuration of 1s . There are no more/ further electrons to lose. Ignore just helium only having two outer electrons (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • cross for electron 1 significantly below those already present • cross for electron 2 slightly below those already present • crosses for electrons 6 to 9 on an approximately straight line upwards continuing from electrons 3 to 5 • cross for electron 10 significantly above the cross for electron 9 and cross for electron 11 slightly above the cross for electron 10 (1) (1) (1) (1) Mark independently Ignore lines drawn between crosses If M3 lost due to electron 9 being too high then allow M4 for correct position of 10 and 11 being significantly above electron 8 Example of sketch (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark following points • there is an increase in atomic radius • (and) there is an increase in shielding/ there is an increase in electron repulsion (by inner shells of electrons) • (resulting in) less attraction between the nucleus and the outer electron(s) • despite the increasing number of protons/ nuclear charge (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow more shells Allow outer electron further from the nucleus Allow electrons in a higher energy level Ignore less energy to remove an electron Do not award if reason for less attraction is decreased number of protons Ignore atomic number (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark • (for sulfur) the outermost electron is paired in the p orbital • so is repelled by the other electron (reducing the first ionisation energy) or • phosphorus is higher because the electron is being removed from a singly occupied p orbital • so there is no electron repulsion (resulting in a higher first ionisation energy) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow electro

parts.answer: • conversion of temperature to K • rearrangement of ideal gas equation • evaluation to give volume • conversion of volume to cm and answer to 2/3 SF (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation T = (108+273 =) 381 (K) Allow addition of 273.15 for conversion V = nRT ÷ p or V = (0.0200 × 8.31 × 381 ÷ 1.28 × 10 =) V = 4.9470 × 10 −4 (m ) V = (4.9470 × 10 −4 × 10 = ) 494.70 (cm ) V = 495/ 490 / 4.95 × 10 cm Correct answer with some working scores (4) TE at each stage Do not award 494 for M4 as incorrect rounding (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • a sigma bond has a single area of orbital overlap and a pi bond has two areas of orbital overlap • a sigma bond has head-on orbital overlap and a pi bond has sideways overlap (1) (1) Suitable labelled diagrams can score both marks Example of diagram Unlabelled diagram showing overlap would score M1 only If no other mark awarded then give (1) for two correct statements about either sigma or pi bonds (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • lack of (free) rotation/ restricted rotation about C=C (1) Allow restricted rotation about the double bond Allow the double bond cannot rotate/ no rotation about the double bond Ignore reference to the groups attached to the double bond Do not award reference to molecular rotation (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • formula of E isomer • formula of Z isomer • formula of isomer with no E-Z isomerism • name of isomer All 4 bullet points scores (3) 3 bullet points scores (2) 2 bullet points scores (1) Allow structural/skeletal/semi-displayed formulae Example of formulae E isomer Z isomer Allow (1 bullet point) for isomers drawn the wrong way round Ignore connectivity 2-methylpropene but-1-ene Allow (2-) methylprop-1-ene Allow 2 methlypropene Penalise missing hydrogen atoms once only (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • formula of dibrominated product top left • formula of diol product bottom middle (reagents top right) • hydrogen (gas)/H and nickel/Ni /platinum/Pt (1) (1)
